metadata.description: Docker 容器: Mirror running instances. run/exec/stop/rm. Port mapping -p. Volume mounting -v. Here are the corresponding brief Python code examples for some of these concepts, assuming we're working with Docker in a Python environment using the `docker` library: ```python # Importing the docker library import docker client = docker.from_env() # Running an instance container = client.containers.run("nginx", detach=True) print(container.id) # Executing a command inside the container exec_id = client.containers.get(container.id).exec_run("echo 'Hello, World!'") print(exec_id.output.decode()) # Stopping the container client.containers.get(container.id).stop() # Removing the container client.containers.get(container.id).remove() # Port mapping -p container_with_ports = client.containers.run("nginx", ports={"80/tcp": 8080}, detach=True) print(container_with_ports.ports) # Volume mounting -v from docker.models.mounts import Mount mount = Mount(source="my_volume", target="/data", type="bind") container_with_volumes = client.containers.run("nginx", mounts=[mount], detach=True) print(container_with_volumes.attrs["Mounts"]) ``` Note: The `docker` library is a Python wrapper for the Docker API and must be installed in your environment (`pip install docker`).

data_pointers.content_preview: Docker 容器: Mirror running instances. run/exec/stop/rm. Port mapping -p. Volume mounting -v. Here are the corresponding brief Python code examples for some of these concepts, assuming we're working with Docker in a Python environment using the `docker` library: ```python # Importing the docker library import docker client = docker.from_env() # Running an instance container = client.containers.run("nginx", detach=True) print(container.id) # Executing a command inside the container exec_id = client.containers.get(container.id).exec_run("echo 'Hello, World!'") print(exec_id.output.decode()) # Stopping the container client.containers.get(container.id).stop() # Removing the container client.containers.get(container.id).remove() # Port mapping -p container_with_ports = client.containers.run("nginx", ports={"80/tcp": 8080}, detach=True) print(container_with_ports.ports) # Volume mounting -v from docker.models.mounts import Mount mount = Mount(source="my_volume", target="/data", type="bind") container_with_volumes = client.containers.run("nginx", mounts=[mount], detach=True) print(container_with_volumes.attrs["Mounts"]) ``` Note: The `docker` library is a Python wrapper for the Docker API and must be installed in your environment (`pip install docker`).
